ffmpeg_encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual ffmpeg_encoder.h(3)

NAME

ffmpeg_encoder.h - FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char *output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t **out)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const uint8_t *rgb, int pitch, uint64_t captured_ns)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const float *samples, int num_samples)
void ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

Overview

The FFmpeg encoder provides a simple interface for encoding video frames or images to disk. The encoder automatically detects the output format and codec from the file extension, handling all necessary color space conversions and encoder configuration.

Input format: RGB24 (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per pixel) Output: File on disk with automatic format/codec detection

Supported Formats & Codecs

Extension Container Video Codec Audio Codec Pixel Format Use Case .mp4, .mov MP4 H.264 AAC YUV420P General streaming, web playback .mkv Matroska H.264 AAC YUV420P High-quality archival, flexible codecs .webm WebM VP9 Opus YUV420P Web-optimized streaming, best compression .avi AVI MPEG-4 PCM YUV420P Legacy format, older systems .flv Flash H.264 AAC YUV420P Flash video, streaming history .ogv, .ogg Ogg Theora Vorbis YUV420P Free/open-source streaming .3gp 3GP H.264 AAC YUV420P Mobile devices, low bitrate .gif GIF GIF None Palette Animated clips, social media .png Image PNG None RGB24 Lossless still images, single frame .jpg, .jpeg Image MJPEG None YUVJ420P Lossy still images, single frame

Default format: MP4 (if extension not recognized) Audio: Automatically selected based on container; image formats are audio-free

Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs

Automatic Bitrate Calculation:

  • Based on resolution: ~1 Mbps per megapixel
  • Range: 500 kbps (minimum) to 5000 kbps (maximum)
  • Formula: bitrate_kbps = (width × height) / 1024, clamped to [500, 5000]
  • Provides reasonable quality for most use cases without manual tuning

Color Space Conversion:

  • Input: RGB24 (24-bit color, standard in graphics/video capture)
  • Conversion method: libswscale with SWS_BICUBIC algorithm
  • SWS_BICUBIC: Bicubic interpolation offering good quality-to-speed balance
  • Pixel format selection:
•
YUV420P: Video formats (H.264, VP9, MPEG-4) — standard ITU-R BT.601 conversion
•
YUVJ420P: JPEG-based formats (MJPEG) — uses full-range YUV (0-255)
•
RGB24: Lossless formats (PNG, GIF) — preserves exact RGB values

Quality vs. Performance:

  • H.264: Best compression, moderate speed (suitable for streaming)
  • VP9: Better quality/size ratio, slower encoding (smaller files)
  • PNG: Lossless, larger files, very slow encoding (use for critical frames)
  • GIF: Lossless animation, severely limited palette (max 256 colors)
  • MJPEG: Fast single-frame capture, reasonable compression

Single-Frame Image Handling:

  • PNG and JPEG formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) output only one frame
  • Multiple ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame() calls write subsequent frames to the same file
  • Useful for snapshots or periodic frame capture

Usage Examples

Encode video to MP4 (default format):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.mp4", 1920, 1080, 30, &enc);
if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    // Handle error
}
// Write frames (RGB24 format, pitch in bytes per row)
uint8_t *frame_rgb = get_frame_data();
int pitch = 1920 * 3;  // 1920 pixels × 3 bytes per pixel
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame_rgb, pitch);
// Cleanup and finalize file
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

High-quality WebM encoding (better compression than H.264):

// Simply use .webm extension
ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.webm", 1920, 1080, 24, &enc);
// ... write frames ...
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

Capture single frame to PNG (lossless snapshot):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("snapshot.png", 1920, 1080, 1, &enc);
uint8_t *frame = capture_frame();
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 1920 * 3);
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes and closes file

Animated GIF from frame sequence:

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("animation.gif", 640, 480, 10, &enc);  // 10 FPS
for (int i = 0; i < num_frames; i++) {


    uint8_t *frame = get_frame(i);


    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 640 * 3);
}
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes GIF with palette

Common Pitfalls & Best Practices

1. Pitch/Stride Parameter:

  • pitch is the byte offset from one row to the next
  • For tightly-packed RGB: pitch = width × 3
  • For aligned/padded buffers: use actual row size (may be larger)
  • Must match the actual memory layout of the input buffer

2. Memory Management:

  • Input frame data is NOT owned by encoder — caller must manage
  • Output file is owned by encoder and written/closed by ffmpeg_encoder_destroy()
  • All resources freed in ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() — always call it

3. Error Recovery:

  • Encoder is NOT usable after any error from ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame()
  • Must call ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() to clean up and finalize file
  • If file operations fail, file may be partially written

4. File Extension Sensitivity:

  • Extension matching is case-sensitive on Unix-like systems
  • Use lowercase extensions (.mp4 not .MP4) for maximum compatibility
  • Unknown extension defaults to MP4 H.264

5. Platform Support:

  • This encoder is NOT AVAILABLE on Windows (_WIN32)
  • Only POSIX systems (Linux, macOS) are supported

Performance Considerations

Encoding Speed (relative):

  • Fastest: GIF, MJPEG (lossless single-frame)
  • Fast: H.264 with default bitrate
  • Medium: VP9 (better compression at cost of speed)
  • Slow: PNG (lossless color preservation)

File Size (for 1080p @ 30fps, 10 seconds):

  • MP4 H.264: ~50-100 MB (depends on bitrate)
  • WebM VP9: ~30-60 MB (better compression)
  • AVI MPEG-4: ~100-150 MB (less efficient compression)
  • PNG: ~100+ MB per frame (lossless, single frame only)
  • GIF: ~200+ MB for 300 frames (palette limited)

Bitrate Impact on Quality:

  • Default bitrate is calculated for acceptable quality at various resolutions
  • For critical content (e.g., document sharing), consider PNG for single frames
  • For streaming, MP4 H.264 offers best balance of quality, compatibility, and size
  • VP9 recommended for highest quality requirements when file size isn't critical

Definition in file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Definition at line 163 of file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char * output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t ** out)

Create and initialize a video/image encoder.

The output format and codec are automatically determined from the file extension. See Supported Formats & Codecs for supported formats.

Bitrate: Automatically calculated from resolution (~1 Mbps per megapixel, clamped to 500-5000 kbps).

Color Space: Input is expected in RGB24 format. Internal conversion to the appropriate output pixel format (YUV420P for video, RGB24 for PNG, YUVJ420P for JPEG) is handled automatically.

Parameters

output_path Path to the output file (extension determines format and codec)
width_px Video width in pixels (must be > 0)
height_px Video height in pixels (must be > 0)
fps Frames per second (must be > 0)
out Pointer to encoder handle (set on success)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The encoder acquires resources (file handles, memory buffers) that must be released with ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(). Failure to do so may leak file handles and memory.

See also

ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame, ffmpeg_encoder_destroy, ffmpeg_encoder_formats

Definition at line 337 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.

338                                                                 {
339   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] START: output=%s, %dx%d @ %dfps", output_path, width_px, height_px, fps);
340 
341   if (!output_path || !out || width_px <= 0 || height_px <= 0)
342     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_create: invalid parameters");
343 
344   // Use default FPS if not specified (0 from config means "use default")
345   if (fps <= 0) {
346     fps = 60;
347     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: fps was 0, using default 60");
348   }
349 
350   ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*enc), ffmpeg_encoder_t *);
351   enc->width_px = width_px;
352   enc->height_px = height_px;
353 
354   // For snapshot mode, calculate output FPS based on input FPS and snapshot duration
355   // If we capture for N seconds at input_fps, we'll get ~(N * input_fps) frames
356   // Output duration should be snapshot_delay seconds
357   // So output_fps = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
358   // EXCEPT: if the input was probed, fps parameter is the actual input fps
359   // Then output_fps should equal input_fps to make frame durations sum correctly
360   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
361   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
362   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
363             snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
364 
365   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
366     // For snapshot mode: if we capture at input_fps for snapshot_delay seconds,
367     // we get approximately (input_fps * snapshot_delay) frames
368     // To make output span exactly snapshot_delay seconds:
369     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
370     // So we should use input_fps as output_fps!
371     // Example: 30fps input for 3s → ~90 frames → output at 30fps → 90/30 = 3 seconds ✓
372 
373     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
374              snapshot_delay, fps);
375     log_info("  → Will estimate ~%.0f frames, output FPS=%d makes durations sum to %.1f seconds",
376              fps * snapshot_delay, fps, snapshot_delay);
377   }
378 
379   enc->fps = fps;
380 
381   // Snapshot mode frame distribution setup
382   enc->estimated_frame_count = 0;
383   enc->estimated_frame_duration = 0;
384   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0 && fps > 0) {
385     // Estimate how many frames we'll capture
386     enc->estimated_frame_count = (int)(fps * snapshot_delay + 0.5);
387     // Each frame should have uniform duration in stream time_base (1/90000 for MP4)
388     // frame_duration = total_duration / estimated_frame_count
389     //                = snapshot_delay seconds * time_base.den / estimated_frame_count
390     // We'll set this to stream->time_base later after it's initialized
391     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode ENABLED - estimated_frame_count=%d (fps=%d * snapshot_delay=%.2f)",
392               enc->estimated_frame_count, fps, snapshot_delay);
393   } else {
394     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode disabled or invalid - snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.2f, fps=%d",
395              snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
396   }
397   enc->is_stdout_pipe = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) ? 1 : 0;
398 
399   // For codec/format detection, use output_path unless it's "-" (stdout)
400   // For stdout, use "fake.mp4" to default to MP4 format
401   const char *detection_path = output_path;
402   if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
403     detection_path = "fake.mp4"; // Fake path for format detection when piping to stdout
404     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using MP4 format for stdout piping");
405   }
406 
407   const char *codec_name = NULL, *format_name = NULL;
408   get_codec_from_extension(detection_path, &codec_name, &format_name, &enc->is_image, &enc->target_pix_fmt);
409 
410   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: %s (%s, %dx%d @ %dfps, %s)", output_path, codec_name, width_px, height_px, fps,
411             enc->is_image ? "image" : "video");
412 
413   // Allocate output media context
414   int ret = avformat_alloc_output_context2(&enc->fmt_ctx, NULL, format_name, output_path);
415   if (ret < 0) {
416     SAFE_FREE(enc);
417     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_alloc_output_context2 failed");
418   }
419 
420   // Find encoder
421   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name(codec_name);
422   if (!codec) {
423     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
424     SAFE_FREE(enc);
425     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: encoder '%s' not found", codec_name);
426   }
427 
428   // Create new stream
429   enc->stream = avformat_new_stream(enc->fmt_ctx, codec);
430   if (!enc->stream) {
431     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
432     SAFE_FREE(enc);
433     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_new_stream failed");
434   }
435 
436   // Allocate codec context
437   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
438   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
439     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
440     SAFE_FREE(enc);
441     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_alloc_context3 failed");
442   }
443 
444   // Set codec parameters
445   enc->codec_ctx->width = width_px;
446   enc->codec_ctx->height = height_px;
447   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = enc->target_pix_fmt;
448   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, fps};
449   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){fps, 1};
450 
451   // Set bitrate for high quality (music-level quality)
452   // Use ~5-10 Mbps per megapixel for visually lossless quality
453   int bitrate = (width_px * height_px * 10) / 1024; // higher quality estimate in kbps
454   if (bitrate < 4000)
455     bitrate = 4000; // Minimum 4000 kbps for quality
456   if (bitrate > 50000)
457     bitrate = 50000; // Cap at 50 Mbps max
458   enc->codec_ctx->bit_rate = bitrate * 1000;
459   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: bitrate set to %d kbps for %dx%d", bitrate, width_px, height_px);
460 
461   // Configure codec-specific options for quality
462   AVDictionary *codec_opts = NULL;
463   if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx264") == 0) {
464     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0);
465     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "28", 0);
466     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x264 preset=ultrafast crf=28");
467   } else if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx265") == 0) {
468     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0);
469     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "32", 0);
470     // x265 tag must be hvc1 for MP4 container compatibility (Apple/browser playback)
471     enc->stream->codecpar->codec_tag = MKTAG('h', 'v', 'c', '1');
472     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x265 preset=ultrafast crf=32");
473   }
474 
475   // Open codec (capture libx264 startup logs)
476   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
477     ret = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, &codec_opts);
478   });
479   av_dict_free(&codec_opts);
480   if (ret < 0) {
481     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
482     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
483     SAFE_FREE(enc);
484     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_open2 failed");
485   }
486 
487   // Copy codec parameters to stream
488   ret = avcodec_parameters_from_context(enc->stream->codecpar, enc->codec_ctx);
489   if (ret < 0) {
490     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
491     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
492     SAFE_FREE(enc);
493     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_parameters_from_context failed");
494   }
495 
496   // Muxer options for proper MP4 container structure
497   AVDictionary *opts = NULL;
498 
499   // Muxer flags configuration
500   // For stdout/non-seekable output, use fragmented MP4 (frag_keyframe)
501   // For regular files, use faststart (moov at front for streaming)
502   bool is_stdout = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0);
503   if (is_stdout) {
504     // Use fragmented MP4 for stdout - starts a new fragment at each video keyframe
505     // This allows streaming without requiring file seeking (FFmpeg docs recommended)
506     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "+frag_keyframe", 0);
507     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using fragmented MP4 (+frag_keyframe) for stdout output");
508   } else {
509     // Move moov atom to the front (faststart) - helps with streaming and player compatibility
510     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "faststart", 0);
511   }
512 
513   if (enc->is_image) {
514     // Use -update flag for single frame output
515     av_dict_set(&opts, "update", "1", 0);
516   }
517 
518   // Allocate frames
519   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
520   enc->frame_encoded = av_frame_alloc();
521   enc->pkt = av_packet_alloc();
522   if (!enc->frame || !enc->frame_encoded || !enc->pkt) {
523     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
524     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
525     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
526     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
527     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
528     SAFE_FREE(enc);
529     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: frame allocation failed");
530   }
531 
532   // Set frame properties for target pixel format
533   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
534   enc->frame_encoded->width = width_px;
535   enc->frame_encoded->height = height_px;
536   ret = av_frame_get_buffer(enc->frame_encoded, 32);
537   if (ret < 0) {
538     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
539     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
540     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
541     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
542     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
543     SAFE_FREE(enc);
544     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: av_frame_get_buffer failed");
545   }
546 
547   // Create SWS context for RGB → target format conversion
548   LOG_IO("swscaler", {
549     enc->sws_ctx = sws_getContext(width_px, height_px, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, width_px, height_px, enc->target_pix_fmt,
550                                   SWS_BICUBIC, NULL, NULL, NULL);
551   });
552   if (!enc->sws_ctx) {
553     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
554     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
555     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
556     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
557     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
558     SAFE_FREE(enc);
559     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: sws_getContext failed");
560   }
561 
562   // Initialize audio stream if supported
563   encoder_init_audio_stream(enc, output_path);
564 
565   // Open output file or stdout
566   if (!(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE)) {
567     // For stdout output, use "pipe:1" (FFmpeg's standard for writing to stdout)
568     const char *open_path = output_path;
569     if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
570       open_path = "pipe:1"; // Use pipe:1 for stdout
571       log_debug("ffmpeg: Redirecting '-' to 'pipe:1' for stdout output");
572     }
573     ret = avio_open(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb, open_path, AVIO_FLAG_WRITE);
574     if (ret < 0) {
575       sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
576       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
577       av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
578       av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
579       avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
580       avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
581       SAFE_FREE(enc);
582       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avio_open failed for '%s'", open_path);
583     }
584   }
585 
586   // Write header (capture FFmpeg format muxer logs)
587   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
588     ret = avformat_write_header(enc->fmt_ctx, &opts);
589   });
590   av_dict_free(&opts); // Free options after use
591   if (ret < 0) {
592     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
593     sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
594     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
595     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
596     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
597     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
598     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
599     SAFE_FREE(enc);
600     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_write_header failed");
601   }
602 
603   // Now that stream->time_base is set, calculate estimated frame duration for snapshot mode
604   bool snapshot_mode_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
605   double snapshot_delay_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
606   if (snapshot_mode_after_header && enc->estimated_frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay_after_header > 0) {
607     // Each frame should span: snapshot_delay / estimated_frame_count seconds
608     // In stream time_base units: (snapshot_delay * time_base.den) / estimated_frame_count
609     enc->estimated_frame_duration =
610         (int64_t)((snapshot_delay_after_header * (double)enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->estimated_frame_count);
611     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Calculated estimated_frame_duration=%lld time_base units "
612               "for snapshot mode (snapshot_delay=%.2f, estimated_frames=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
613               (long long)enc->estimated_frame_duration, snapshot_delay_after_header, enc->estimated_frame_count,
614               enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den);
615   }
616 
617   enc->frame_count = 0;
618   enc->video_pts = 0;
619   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = 0.0;
620   enc->snapshot_elapsed_ns = 0;
621   enc->first_frame_captured_ns = 0;  // Initialize first frame timestamp
622   *out = enc;
623 
624   /* Register encoder with named registry */
625   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER(enc, output_path, NULL);
626 
627   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] SUCCESS: encoder initialized, frame_count=0, fps=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->fps, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
628   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
629 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_image,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe, log_debug, log_info,
    LOG_IO, NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SET_ERRNO, ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_elapsed_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc)


Close and finalize the output file, releasing all encoder
    resources.




This function flushes any pending frames in the encoder, writes
    the file trailer, and releases all allocated resources (memory buffers, file
    handles, codecs).




Critical: This MUST be called to properly finalize the
    output file. Without this call, the file may be incomplete or
  unplayable.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (currently always succeeds)


Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (returns ASCIICHAT_OK).



After this call, enc must not be used again.




The output file is NOT guaranteed to be complete until this
    function returns.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create


Definition at line 881 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


881                                                                 {
882   if (!enc)
883     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
884 
885   NAMED_UNREGISTER(enc);
886 
887   // In snapshot mode, calculate the FPS needed to match the target duration
888   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
889   int64_t adjusted_packet_duration = 0;
890   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
891 
892   if (snapshot_mode && enc->frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay > 0) {
893     // Calculate output FPS that distributes frames across snapshot_delay seconds
894     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay
895     // packet_duration (in stream time_base) = stream_time_base.den / output_fps
896     //                                        = (stream_time_base.den * snapshot_delay) / frame_count
897     adjusted_packet_duration = (int64_t)((snapshot_delay * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
898     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - adjusting packet durations for %d frames over %.2f seconds",
899              enc->frame_count, snapshot_delay);
900     log_debug("  Adjusted packet duration: %lld units (%.6f seconds per frame)",
901               (long long)adjusted_packet_duration,
902               (double)adjusted_packet_duration / enc->stream->time_base.den);
903   }
904 
905   // Flush video encoder (capture libx264 final statistics)
906   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
907     avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, NULL);
908     int flush_pkt_count = 0;
909     while (1) {
910       int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
911       if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
912         break;
913       if (ret < 0)
914         break;
915 
916       flush_pkt_count++;
917 
918       // Set packet duration in codec time base if not already set
919       if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
920         enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
921       }
922 
923       // In snapshot mode, rescale to stream time_base and apply adjusted duration if set
924       int64_t duration_before = enc->pkt->duration;
925       av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
926       if (snapshot_mode && adjusted_packet_duration > 0) {
927         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_packet_duration;
928         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flush packet %d duration adjusted from %lld to %lld",
929                   flush_pkt_count, (long long)duration_before, (long long)adjusted_packet_duration);
930       }
931       enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
932       av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
933       av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
934     }
935   });
936 
937   // Flush audio encoder if present
938   if (enc->has_audio_stream && enc->audio_codec_ctx) {
939     // Write any remaining partial audio samples as padding
940     if (enc->audio_partial_len > 0) {
941       // Zero-pad the remaining samples
942       memset(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], 0,
943              (enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len) * sizeof(float));
944       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
945       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
946       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
947       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
948       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
949     }
950 
951     // Flush any remaining packets (capture FFmpeg audio codec logs)
952     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
953       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, NULL);
954       while (1) {
955         int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
956         if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
957           break;
958         if (ret < 0)
959           break;
960 
961         av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
962         enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
963         av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
964         av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
965       }
966     });
967   }
968 
969   // Set stream duration for proper metadata (must be before trailer)
970   if (enc->stream && enc->frame_count > 0) {
971     int64_t duration;
972     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
973     if (snapshot_mode) {
974       extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
975       double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
976       // Use actual capture elapsed time if available, otherwise use snapshot_delay
977       // This ensures frames are distributed across the correct time window
978       double actual_capture_duration = g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
979       double effective_duration = (actual_capture_duration > 0) ? actual_capture_duration : snapshot_delay;
980 
981       // In snapshot mode with wall-clock timing, the video duration should equal the actual elapsed time
982       // duration_seconds = effective_duration
983       // duration_time_base_units = effective_duration * time_base.den
984       duration = (int64_t)(effective_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den);
985 
986       // Calculate what the frame durations should be to sum to effective_duration
987       // This ensures FFmpeg plays the captured frames across the full effective_duration
988       // effective_frame_duration = effective_duration / frame_count (seconds per frame)
989       int64_t effective_frame_duration = (int64_t)((effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count) * enc->stream->time_base.den);
990 
991       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - snapshot_delay=%.2f, frames=%d, output_duration=%.2f sec",
992                snapshot_delay, enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
993       log_debug("  Using effective_duration=%.2f seconds for output", effective_duration);
994       log_debug("  stream->time_base=%d/%d, effective frame duration=%lld time_base units",
995                 enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den, (long long)effective_frame_duration);
996       log_debug("  (each frame should span %.3f seconds to total %.2f seconds)",
997                 effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
998 
999       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Setting stream->duration=%lld (effective_duration=%.3f, time_base=%d/%d, equiv=%.3f sec)",
1000                (long long)duration, effective_duration,
1001                enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den,
1002                (double)duration * enc->stream->time_base.num / enc->stream->time_base.den);
1003     } else {
1004       // Normal mode: calculate duration from frame count and FPS
1005       // time_base = 1 / time_base.den seconds per unit
1006       // Each frame is 1/fps seconds, so duration = frame_count / fps seconds
1007       // In time base units: duration = (frame_count / fps) * time_base.den = frame_count * time_base.den / fps
1008       duration = (int64_t)enc->frame_count * enc->stream->time_base.den / enc->fps;
1009       log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Normal mode - Set stream duration=%lld (frames=%d, fps=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
1010                 (long long)duration, enc->frame_count, enc->fps, 1, enc->stream->time_base.den);
1011     }
1012     enc->stream->duration = duration;
1013 
1014     // CRITICAL: Adjust last frame duration to ensure total duration matches stream->duration
1015     // Each frame was encoded with FPS-based duration, but snapshot mode needs them distributed
1016     // across the actual capture duration. Since we can't change past frame durations, we extend
1017     // the last frame's duration to make the sum equal the desired total.
1018     if (snapshot_mode) {
1019       // Calculate sum of all frame durations (each frame currently has the same FPS-based duration)
1020       int64_t current_total_duration = enc->video_pts;  // This is the sum of all frame durations so far
1021       int64_t desired_duration = duration;
1022 
1023       // The last frame needs to be extended to: desired - (sum of all other frames)
1024       // This ensures the video plays for exactly the snapshot_delay duration
1025       int64_t last_frame_extension = desired_duration - current_total_duration;
1026 
1027       if (last_frame_extension > 0) {
1028         // We need to extend the last frame. But we can't modify it directly after encoding.
1029         // Instead, the muxer will use stream->duration as the authoritative source.
1030         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Last frame would need extension of %lld units to reach total %lld (currently %lld)",
1031                   (long long)last_frame_extension, (long long)desired_duration, (long long)current_total_duration);
1032       }
1033     }
1034   }
1035 
1036   // Write trailer (capture FFmpeg muxer logs and final frame statistics)
1037   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1038     av_write_trailer(enc->fmt_ctx);
1039   });
1040 
1041   // Flush output buffer for stdout/pipes before closing
1042   if (enc->fmt_ctx && enc->fmt_ctx->pb) {
1043     avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1044     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flushed output buffer");
1045   }
1046 
1047   // Close output file
1048   if (enc->fmt_ctx && !(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE))
1049     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1050 
1051   // Cleanup
1052   sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
1053   av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
1054   av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
1055 
1056   // Cleanup audio resources (capture any remaining FFmpeg logs from codec cleanup)
1057   if (enc->has_audio_stream) {
1058     swr_free(&enc->audio_swr_ctx);
1059     av_frame_free(&enc->audio_frame);
1060     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1061       avcodec_free_context(&enc->audio_codec_ctx);
1062     });
1063     SAFE_FREE(enc->audio_partial_buf);
1064   }
1065 
1066   av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
1067   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1068     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
1069   });
1070   if (enc->fmt_ctx)
1071     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
1072 
1073   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: wrote %d frames", enc->frame_count);
1074   SAFE_FREE(enc);
1075   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1076 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_swr_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_debug,
    log_info, LOG_IO, NAMED_UNREGISTER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_FREE,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts.


Referenced by render_file_destroy().






void
  ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc,
  double actual_duration_sec)


Set the actual wall-clock duration for snapshot mode frame
  timing.




In snapshot mode, frames are captured over a wall-clock duration
    (e.g., 3 seconds of real time). This function should be called after all
    frames are captured to provide the actual elapsed time, allowing the encoder
    to calculate correct frame durations so the output video duration matches
    the actual capture time.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


actual_duration_sec Actual wall-clock duration in seconds (e.g., 3.5 for
  3.5 seconds)


Note


Only used in snapshot mode. In normal mode, this is
  ignored.



Should be called before ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() for the
    adjustment to take effect.




If not called, frame durations are calculated from FPS, which may
    not match actual wall-clock capture time.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 862 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


862                                                                                                     {
863   if (!enc)
864     return;
865   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = actual_duration_sec;
866 
867   // If we already have frame count, we can calculate the adjusted packet duration now
868   // This will be used for any remaining frames and during flush
869   if (enc->frame_count > 0 && actual_duration_sec > 0) {
870     // Calculate the packet duration that would make all frames fit in actual_duration_sec
871     // packet_duration_units = (actual_duration_sec * time_base.den) / frame_count
872     // This is only a starting point - the actual duration might be different if more frames are captured
873     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: %.3f sec with %d frames so far",
874               actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
875   } else {
876     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: Setting duration to %.3f seconds (already encoded %d frames)",
877             actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
878   }
879 }






References ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count, log_debug,
    log_info, and ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration.


Referenced by
  render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const float * samples, int num_samples)


Write audio samples to the output file.




Encodes and writes audio samples to the output file. Audio samples
    must be in 48kHz mono float32 format. The function internally buffers and
    accumulates samples into codec frames before encoding.




Audio Format Requirements:


    
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz (48 kHz)
    • 
  
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
    • 
  
  • Format: 32-bit IEEE float (AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT)
    • 



Notes:


    
  
  • Audio is only written if the output format supports audio (MP4, WebM,
      AVI).
    • 
  
  • Image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF) ignore audio writes.
    • 
  
  • Samples are buffered internally and encoded in chunks as needed.
    • 
  
  • Timing is automatically synchronized with video using PTS (presentation
      timestamp).
    • 



Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


samples Pointer to audio samples in float32 format (not owned, used
  immediately)


num_samples Number of samples to write (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input sample data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



Safe to call on encoders without audio streams (no-op).




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame


Definition at line 795 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


795                                                                                                            {
796   if (!enc || !samples || num_samples <= 0)
797     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Silently ignore invalid calls
798 
799   // No audio stream for this format
800   if (!enc->has_audio_stream)
801     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
802 
803   // Accumulate samples into partial buffer
804   int samples_to_process = num_samples;
805   int samples_offset = 0;
806 
807   while (samples_to_process > 0) {
808     // How many samples can we add to the partial buffer?
809     int space_in_buffer = enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len;
810     int samples_to_copy = (samples_to_process < space_in_buffer) ? samples_to_process : space_in_buffer;
811 
812     // Copy samples to partial buffer
813     memcpy(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], &samples[samples_offset], samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
814     enc->audio_partial_len += samples_to_copy;
815     samples_offset += samples_to_copy;
816     samples_to_process -= samples_to_copy;
817 
818     // If buffer is full, encode and write frame
819     if (enc->audio_partial_len >= enc->audio_frame_size) {
820       // Set up audio frame with accumulated samples
821       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
822       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
823       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
824       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
825 
826       // Send frame to encoder
827       int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
828       if (ret < 0) {
829         log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame for audio failed");
830       } else {
831         // Receive and write packets
832         while (ret >= 0) {
833           ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
834           if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
835             break;
836           if (ret < 0) {
837             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet for audio failed");
838             break;
839           }
840 
841           // Rescale timestamp and write
842           av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
843           enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
844           ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
845           av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
846           if (ret < 0) {
847             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame for audio failed");
848             break;
849           }
850         }
851       }
852 
853       // Update PTS for next frame
854       enc->audio_pts += enc->audio_frame_size;
855       enc->audio_partial_len = 0;
856     }
857   }
858 
859   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
860 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_warn_every, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const uint8_t * rgb, int pitch,
  uint64_t captured_ns)


Write a single video frame to the output file.




The frame must be in RGB24 format (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per
    pixel). Internal color space conversion is performed automatically based on
    the output format (see Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs for
    details).




Pitch Parameter: The pitch parameter specifies the byte
    offset from one row to the next. For tightly-packed RGB data: pitch =
    width_px × 3. For padded/aligned buffers: pitch must reflect the
    actual row size.




Timestamp Parameter: The captured_ns parameter is the
    wall-clock timestamp (in nanoseconds) when the frame was captured. This
    ensures video playback uses actual capture timing, not encoder processing
    time. For correct frame timing in the output video, captured_ns should be
    the time when the frame was acquired from the media source.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


rgb Pointer to frame data in RGB24 format (not copied, used immediately)


pitch Byte offset between rows (must match actual buffer layout)


captured_ns Wall-clock timestamp in nanoseconds when frame was
  captured


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input frame data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



For image-only formats (PNG, JPEG), only the last frame written is
    kept. Previous frames are discarded when the next frame is written.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 631 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


632                                                                    {
633   if (!enc || !rgb)
634     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: NULL input");
635 
636   // Initialize first frame capture time on first call
637   if (enc->first_frame_captured_ns == 0) {
638     enc->first_frame_captured_ns = captured_ns;
639     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: first frame captured at %llu ns", (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
640   }
641 
642   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_WRITE] frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
643   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d, captured_ns=%llu", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px,
644             enc->height_px, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
645 
646   // Set up RGB frame for conversion
647   uint8_t *data[1] = {(uint8_t *)rgb};
648   int linesize[1] = {pitch};
649 
650   // CRITICAL: Ensure frame format is set before each sws_scale call
651   // Some FFmpeg versions reset the format field, so we need to set it explicitly
652   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
653 
654   // Convert RGB24 to target format
655   sws_scale(enc->sws_ctx, (const uint8_t *const *)data, linesize, 0, enc->height_px, enc->frame_encoded->data,
656             enc->frame_encoded->linesize);
657 
658   // Set frame duration based on mode
659   int64_t frame_duration;
660   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
661 
662   if (enc->frame_count == 0) {
663     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame 0, snapshot_mode=%d", snapshot_mode);
664   }
665 
666   // Use FPS-based frame duration (snapshot mode adjusts total duration in destroy())
667   frame_duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
668 
669   enc->frame_encoded->duration = frame_duration;
670 
671   // Track this frame's capture timestamp for duration calculation of next frame
672   if (snapshot_mode) {
673     uint64_t frame_dur_ms = (enc->previous_captured_ns > 0) ? (captured_ns - enc->previous_captured_ns) / 1000000 : 0;
674     if (enc->frame_count <= 2) {
675       log_info("ffmpeg: frame %d SET duration=%lld units (%.3fms since last, codec_base=1/%d)",
676                enc->frame_count, (long long)frame_duration, (double)frame_dur_ms,
677                enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den);
678     }
679     enc->previous_captured_ns = captured_ns;
680   }
681 
682   // Calculate PTS from actual capture timestamp
683   // time_base is {1, fps}, so 1 PTS unit = 1/fps seconds
684   // Convert elapsed nanoseconds to PTS units: elapsed_ns * fps / 1_000_000_000
685   uint64_t elapsed_ns = captured_ns - enc->first_frame_captured_ns;
686   int64_t pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)((elapsed_ns * (uint64_t)enc->fps) / 1000000000ULL);
687 
688   // In snapshot mode, distribute frames linearly across the actual duration
689   if (snapshot_mode) {
690     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
691     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
692     if (g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms > 0) {
693       // Distribute frames linearly based on their capture timestamp relative to total duration
694       // pts = (frame_elapsed_ns / total_elapsed_ns) * actual_duration_sec * fps
695       // This ensures frame 0 is at 0s and last frame is at actual_duration_sec
696       double actual_duration_sec = (double)g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
697       double total_elapsed_sec = (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
698 
699       if (total_elapsed_sec > 0.001) {
700         double frame_fraction = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
701         double target_pts_sec = frame_fraction * actual_duration_sec;
702         int64_t pts_before = pts_from_timestamp;
703         // Convert target seconds to time_base units: pts = target_sec * time_base.den / time_base.num
704         pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)(target_pts_sec * (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num);
705 
706         if (enc->frame_count < 3 || enc->frame_count % 10 == 0) {
707           log_debug("ffmpeg: snapshot frame %d: PTS distributed (before=%lld, after=%lld, sec=%lld->%lld, frac=%.3f)",
708                     enc->frame_count, (long long)pts_before, (long long)pts_from_timestamp,
709                     (long long)((double)pts_before * enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num / enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den),
710                     (long long)target_pts_sec, frame_fraction);
711         }
712       }
713     }
714   }
715 
716   // Use timestamp-based (or scaled) PTS
717   enc->frame_encoded->pts = pts_from_timestamp;
718   enc->video_pts += frame_duration;
719 
720   if (!snapshot_mode) {
721     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: PTS calculation - elapsed_ns=%llu, pts_from_timestamp=%lld, fps=%d",
722               (unsigned long long)elapsed_ns, (long long)pts_from_timestamp, enc->fps);
723   }
724 
725   // Send frame to encoder
726   int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, enc->frame_encoded);
727   if (ret < 0) {
728     log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame failed");
729     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Continue anyway
730   }
731 
732   // Receive packets from encoder and write to file
733   static int write_frame_pkt_count = 0;
734 
735   while (ret >= 0) {
736     ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
737     if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
738       break;
739     if (ret < 0) {
740       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet failed");
741       break;
742     }
743 
744     write_frame_pkt_count++;
745 
746     // Set packet duration in codec time base (duration of one frame at target FPS)
747     // This ensures proper stream duration calculation
748     if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
749       enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
750     }
751 
752     av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
753     enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
754 
755     // In snapshot mode, apply adjusted duration if snapshot_actual_duration is set
756     // This happens when we get the actual duration from the capture thread
757     bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
758     if (is_snapshot_mode && enc->snapshot_actual_duration > 0 && enc->frame_count > 0) {
759       int64_t adjusted_dur = (int64_t)((enc->snapshot_actual_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
760       // Only apply if it's significantly different from the default
761       if (adjusted_dur > enc->pkt->duration * 2) {
762         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_dur;
763         log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame packet %d duration adjusted to %lld units based on actual duration",
764                   write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration);
765       }
766     } else if (!is_snapshot_mode && write_frame_pkt_count < 3) {
767       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d, duration=%lld, snapshot_mode=%d, actual_dur=%.2f, frame_count=%d",
768                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration, is_snapshot_mode, enc->snapshot_actual_duration,
769                 enc->frame_count);
770     }
771 
772     if (write_frame_pkt_count <= 2 || write_frame_pkt_count >= 16) {
773       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d with final duration=%lld (%.6f sec in stream time_base), total frames=%d",
774                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration,
775                 (double)enc->pkt->duration / enc->stream->time_base.den, enc->frame_count);
776     }
777 
778     ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
779     av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
780     if (ret < 0) {
781       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame failed");
782       break;
783     }
784 
785     // Flush output buffer for stdout to ensure frames are written immediately
786     if (enc->is_stdout_pipe) {
787       avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
788     }
789   }
790 
791   enc->frame_count++;
792   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
793 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms,
    g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn_every,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::previous_captured_ns, SET_ERRNO,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().
